helpful: fedcourts stats --court scotus --resolved-only: the disposition shares over the same bulk-import population are the whole prediction for a stub snapshot like this one
fedcourts query --court scotus: prior rows showed how opinion-derived dockets get labeled, confirming 'other' as the modal reconciliation
gaps: The snapshot omits the linked cluster's own disposition/summary fields even though the cluster id is in the snapshot; exposing them (they are point-in-time facts, not new fetches) would move sparse historical cells off pure base rates
stats filters (term, date window) are unusable for dockets with no docket number or dates — a filter keyed on record sparsity (e.g. bulk-import stubs) would give a sharper reference class
notes: Base-rate-only cell: the calibrated call differs from an earlier sibling run that predicted 'denied' before the stats tool existed; the corpus label distribution makes 'other' the defensible mode.
